evals.prompt: Prove the Infinitude of Primes theorem (Easy difficulty). The theorem states: There are infinitely many prime numbers.
evals.expected_output: A concise proof using Euclid's classic argument (contradiction), 3-8 lines, in \begin{proof}...\end{proof} format
expectations: Proof uses contradiction or constructive argument
Proof is concise (under 10 lines of math)
No markdown fences or commentary outside the proof

evals.prompt: Prove the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic (Hard difficulty). The theorem states: Every integer n > 1 can be written uniquely as a product of prime numbers. Dependencies: Division Lemma, Definition of Prime.
evals.expected_output: A thorough proof covering both existence (by strong induction) and uniqueness (using Euclid's lemma), 20+ lines, referencing \ref{lem:division} and \ref{def:prime}
expectations: Proof addresses both existence AND uniqueness
Proof establishes or invokes Euclid's lemma for uniqueness
No markdown fences or commentary outside the proof

evals.prompt: Verify a correct proof of FTA (expect PASS or NEEDS_REVISION, not FAIL)
evals.expected_output: A verdict of PASS or NEEDS_REVISION with constructive feedback
expectations: Issues are tagged with severity levels

evals.prompt: Verify a broken proof of FTA that skips Euclid's lemma (expect FAIL)
evals.expected_output: A verdict of FAIL identifying the missing Euclid's lemma as a critical gap
expectations: Issues mention Euclid's lemma or the unjustified claim that p divides some q_j
At least one [critical] issue is identified

evals.prompt: Sketch a proof strategy for FTA (Hard difficulty)
evals.expected_output: A structured sketch identifying strong induction for existence, Euclid's lemma as a prerequisite for uniqueness, and estimating the proof as long/complex
expectations: Prerequisites identify that Euclid's lemma is needed
